agent.name: runtime-bug-hunter
agent.role: Re-audit hosting clarity (f-e114710d) and repair_jobs GRANT narrowing (f-58c68aa7) on scoped files.

findings.title: Deployment target ambiguity: Netlify config coexists with Vercel project file
findings.description: netlify.toml states Netlify is production hosting and warns against reintroducing apps/dashboard/vercel.json. README architecture names Netlify for the dashboard. apps/dashboard/vercel.json is absent in repo, removing dual-platform drift.

proof_hooks.summary: netlify.toml header: production hosting is Netlify; do not reintroduce vercel.json

history.notes: Re-audit: single source of truth documented; Vercel app config file not present.

findings.title: Broad GRANT on repair_jobs to anon and authenticated
findings.description: Follow-up migration revokes SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE on repair_jobs from anon, narrowing blast radius while keeping authenticated (and service_role) per standard Supabase patterns. Original GRANT in 20260402231116_repair_service.sql remains historical; effective state includes REVOKE.

proof_hooks.summary: REVOKE SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs FROM anon
Original broad GRANT on repair_jobs to anon, authenticated

history.notes: Re-audit: anon table privileges on repair_jobs explicitly revoked in later migration.

next_actions.action: Ensure CI or deploy applies migration 20260407210000 to all environments.
next_actions.rationale: REVOKE must run against production DB for effect.

next_actions.action: If Netlify ever adds Vercel again, reconcile with netlify.toml comment.
